name: otelcol_receiver_accepted_spans_total
definition: Number of spans successfully accepted by the receiver and passed into the pipeline. Primary trace ingest throughput signal.
absence_means: SDK instrumentation not running or collector endpoint misconfigured. Distinguish from refused (backpressure).
cardinality_footguns: context.receiver safe (few values). Avoid context.service.instance.id for group-by (high cardinality).

name: otelcol_receiver_accepted_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points successfully accepted by the receiver. Measures metrics pipeline ingest throughput. Often orders-of-magnitude higher than span count.
absence_means: No metric data is being ingested. Check if receivers are configured and scrape targets are reachable.

name: otelcol_receiver_accepted_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records successfully accepted by the receiver. Measures log pipeline ingest throughput.
absence_means: No log data ingested. Check filelog or OTLP log receiver configuration.

name: otelcol_receiver_refused_spans_total
definition: Number of spans the receiver refused by sending backpressure to the SDK. NOT data loss — the SDK buffers and retries. Sustained non-zero rate indicates downstream congestion propagating upstream.
absence_means: No backpressure on trace ingest (healthy) OR self-telemetry not configured.

name: otelcol_receiver_refused_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points refused by the receiver (backpressure). Same semantics as refused_spans — not data loss.
absence_means: No backpressure on metrics pipeline (healthy).

name: otelcol_receiver_refused_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records refused by the receiver (backpressure). Not data loss.
absence_means: No backpressure on log pipeline (healthy).

name: otelcol_receiver_failed_spans_total
definition: Number of spans that failed at the receiver due to decode/parse errors before entering the pipeline. These are permanently lost. Non-zero rate indicates malformed payloads from the sending SDK.
absence_means: No parse errors (healthy). If absent alongside all other otelcol metrics — self-telemetry disabled.

name: otelcol_receiver_failed_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points that failed at the receiver due to parse/decode errors. Permanently lost.
absence_means: No parse errors (healthy).

name: otelcol_receiver_failed_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records that failed at the receiver due to parse/decode errors. Permanently lost.
absence_means: No parse errors (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_sent_spans_total
definition: Number of spans successfully sent to the backend. Definitive success signal for the trace pipeline. Should closely match receiver_accepted_spans_total in a healthy pipeline.
absence_means: Exporter not configured or backend not reachable (all items failing or queued).

name: otelcol_exporter_sent_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points successfully sent to the backend.
absence_means: Metrics exporter not working or backend unreachable.

name: otelcol_exporter_sent_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records successfully sent to the backend.
absence_means: Log exporter not working or backend unreachable.

name: otelcol_exporter_send_failed_spans_total
definition: Number of spans permanently lost after all export retries were exhausted. This is the primary data loss signal. Any sustained non-zero rate is a critical condition requiring immediate investigation.
absence_means: No data loss on trace pipeline (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_send_failed_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points permanently lost after all export retries. Critical data loss signal for the metrics pipeline.
absence_means: No data loss on metrics pipeline (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_send_failed_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records permanently lost after all export retries. Critical data loss signal for the log pipeline.
absence_means: No data loss on log pipeline (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_spans_total
definition: Number of spans dropped because the exporter queue was full at enqueue time. Occurs before any export attempt — these items never reach the backend. Distinct from send_failed (which exhausted retries).
absence_means: Queue has headroom; no items are being dropped at enqueue (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_enqueue_failed_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records dropped because the exporter queue was full at enqueue time. Companion to enqueue_failed_spans for the log pipeline.
absence_means: No log records dropped at enqueue (healthy).

name: otelcol_exporter_queue_size
definition: Current number of items sitting in the exporter retry/send queue. Oscillating 0-40% of capacity is healthy. Steadily climbing or flat at capacity indicates backend pressure.
absence_means: Exporter queue is empty (items draining quickly — healthy) or exporter not configured.
cardinality_footguns: context.exporter safe. context.data_type disambiguates spans/metrics/logs queue.

name: otelcol_exporter_queue_capacity
definition: Maximum configured capacity of the exporter queue. Use alongside queue_size to compute fill percentage (queue_size / queue_capacity * 100). When full and send_failed rising: active data loss.
absence_means: Exporter queue not configured (rare — default is 1000 for OTLP exporter).

name: otelcol_processor_accepted_spans_total
definition: Number of spans accepted by a processor and entering the processor stage. Useful for identifying which processor is a throughput bottleneck.
absence_means: Processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.

name: otelcol_processor_accepted_metric_points_total
definition: Number of metric data points accepted by a processor.
absence_means: Processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.

name: otelcol_processor_accepted_log_records_total
definition: Number of log records accepted by a processor.
absence_means: Processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.

name: otelcol_processor_dropped_spans
definition: Number of spans explicitly dropped by a processor (e.g., filterprocessor, tailsampling). Non-zero is expected for filter/sampler processors. Alert if non-zero on processors that should pass everything through.
absence_means: No spans are being dropped by any processor (healthy for non-filter processors).
cardinality_footguns: context.processor safe. Non-_total variant; note context.k8s.cluster.name absent.

name: otelcol_processor_dropped_metric_points
definition: Number of metric data points explicitly dropped by a processor.
absence_means: No metric points being dropped by any processor.

name: otelcol_processor_dropped_log_records
definition: Number of log records explicitly dropped by a processor.
absence_means: No log records being dropped by any processor.

name: otelcol_processor_incoming_items_total
definition: Total items (all signal types combined) entering any processor. Generic cross-signal throughput signal. Use with outgoing_items to detect which processor is dropping items.
absence_means: Processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.
cardinality_footguns: context.processor safe. context.otel.signal splits by traces/metrics/logs.

name: otelcol_processor_outgoing_items_total
definition: Total items (all signal types combined) exiting a processor. If incoming > outgoing for a processor, items are being dropped by that processor (expected for filter/sampler; alert for others).
absence_means: Processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.
cardinality_footguns: context.processor safe. context.otel.signal splits by traces/metrics/logs.

name: otelcol_processor_batch_batch_send_size
definition: Distribution of the number of items sent per batch by the batch processor. p95 near configured send_batch_size = healthy (size-driven). p95 far below send_batch_size = timeout-driven flushing (traffic too low or timeout too short).
absence_means: Batch processor not in pipeline or self-telemetry disabled.

name: otelcol_processor_batch_timeout_trigger_send_total
definition: Number of times the batch processor flushed because the send_batch_timeout timer fired (timeout-driven flush). High rate relative to total flushes at normal traffic indicates batch size config is too large for the traffic pattern.
absence_means: Batch processor not in pipeline or all flushes are size-driven (healthy at high traffic).

name: otelcol_processor_batch_metadata_cardinality
definition: Number of distinct metadata (resource attribute) combinations currently tracked by the batch processor. High values (>100) indicate resource attribute explosion causing memory pressure. Each distinct combination gets its own batch bucket.
absence_means: Batch processor not in pipeline or all telemetry shares the same resource attributes (ideal: value = 1).
cardinality_footguns: context.processor safe. If metric itself missing — batch processor not configured.

name: otelcol_process_cpu_seconds_total
definition: CPU time consumed by the collector process in seconds. Delta counter. Use per-second rate to approximate CPU utilization (value near 1.0 = ~1 full CPU core). Split by context.service.instance.id per pod.
absence_means: Process self-telemetry not enabled or collector not running. Check telemetry.metrics.level setting.
cardinality_footguns: context.service.instance.id = collector pod (bounded by pod count).

name: otelcol_process_memory_rss_bytes
definition: Resident set size (RSS) memory used by the collector process in bytes. Key signal for memory_limiter health. Rising toward configured limit triggers refused_* backpressure.
absence_means: Process self-telemetry not enabled. Check telemetry.metrics.level setting.

name: otelcol_process_uptime_seconds_total
definition: Collector process uptime as a delta counter (seconds elapsed per scrape interval). Use sum + per-second to produce an uptime rate: 1.0 = running continuously, <1.0 = recent restart. A drop indicates a pod restart — correlate with send_failed/refused spikes before the reset.
absence_means: Process telemetry not enabled or collector not running.

name: otelcol_process_runtime_heap_alloc_bytes
definition: Go runtime heap memory currently allocated by the collector process. Useful for detecting memory leaks (heap growing without plateau) and correlating with memory_limiter triggers. Requires telemetry.metrics.level: detailed.
absence_means: Detailed telemetry not enabled (requires telemetry.metrics.level: detailed) or metric emitted under different name.

name: otelcol_process_runtime_total_sys_memory_bytes
definition: Total memory obtained from the OS by the Go runtime. Broader than heap_alloc; includes runtime overhead. Use alongside heap_alloc to understand total collector memory footprint.
absence_means: Detailed telemetry not enabled or metric absent in this collector version.

name: NOT_APPLICABLE
definition: OpenTelemetry Collector is an infrastructure pipeline component with no direct cost metrics. Resource usage (CPU/memory) is the closest proxy for cost. Use otelcol_process_cpu_seconds_total and otelcol_process_memory_rss_bytes as compute cost proxies. Consider cardinality reduction (filter processors) to lower downstream storage costs.
absence_means: N/A
